title: Parkio Daily for Tuesday, April 28, 2026
teaser: Halloween party dates drop, Food & Wine Festival returns, and Hyperspace Mountain is back at Disneyland for a limited run.

rides.name: Soarin'
rides.note: Walk-on windows often open during evening hours

rides.name: Guardians of the Galaxy: Cosmic Rewind
Space Mountain (Hyperspace)
rides.note: Limited-time Star Wars overlay now running

bignews.title: Halloween party returns to Magic Kingdom this August
bignews.body: Mickey's Not So Scary Halloween Party tickets are on sale now for select nights starting in late summer. The after-hours event typically sells out in advance, and this year's party will once again feature trick-or-treating, character cavalcades, and the Boo-to-You parade.

parkioInsight.text: Party nights usually mean lighter daytime crowds at Magic Kingdom as guests hold tickets for the separately-ticketed evening. Consider visiting Magic Kingdom on party dates if you're skipping the event—waits often drop after 4pm.

bignews.title: Food & Wine Festival dates confirmed for EPCOT this fall
bignews.body: EPCOT's signature culinary event returns for its 31st year with festival marketplace booths, live entertainment, and specialty menu items. The celebration runs through late fall and transforms World Showcase into a global tasting destination.

parkioInsight.text: World Showcase pathways tend to fill up on festival weekends, especially between 12pm and 8pm. Visiting on weekdays or arriving before 11am usually keeps booth lines under 10 minutes.

bignews.title: Hyperspace Mountain overlay returns to Disneyland for limited run
bignews.body: Space Mountain has shifted back to its Star Wars theme with new projections, music, and a narrative that ties into the original trilogy. The overlay is running now for a limited time ahead of expanded Star Wars character appearances launching tomorrow in Galaxy's Edge.

parkioInsight.text: Hyperspace Mountain tends to draw Star Wars fans early. Riding within the first 90 minutes of park opening usually keeps your wait under 30 minutes, even on busier days.

topstories.title: Carthay Circle Restaurant closing briefly for interior updates
topstories.body: The signature dining location at Disney California Adventure has construction walls around its entrance and will close tomorrow, April 29, reopening April 30. The restaurant is still taking reservations for meals starting April 30.

parkioInsight.text: If you're visiting California Adventure tomorrow and had hoped for a walk-up table, the restaurant will be closed. Storytellers Cafe at the Grand Californian often has same-day availability as an alternative.

topstories.title: Dapper Day returns to Disney World this weekend
topstories.body: The unofficial vintage fashion gathering takes over EPCOT on May 3 as guests dress in retro-inspired outfits for photos, character meets, and socializing. The event is open to anyone—no ticket required beyond regular park admission.

parkioInsight.text: EPCOT's World Showcase may feel busier than usual this Sunday, especially around popular photo spots like the Italy pavilion and Spaceship Earth. Arriving before 10am tends to give you clearer backdrops before the crowd peaks.

icymi.title: Disney partners with Make-A-Wish for Week of Wishes initiative
icymi.body: The Walt Disney Company announced an expanded collaboration with the Make-A-Wish Foundation, granting additional wishes for critically ill children and opening a new Wish Lounge at a Disney resort.

icymi.title: Disney Store previews Halloween costumes ahead of October
icymi.body: Halfway to Halloween brought early reveals of this year's character costumes, including Star Wars, Marvel, and Disney Princess collections. Many popular styles are already available for pre-order online.

spotlight.title: Discovery Cove brings back Paradise Nights summer event after 8-year break
spotlight.body: SeaWorld's all-inclusive sister park will host evening events starting June 5, with happy hour, beachside dining, and tropical entertainment. The separately ticketed experience runs on select nights through August 8 and includes beer, wine, and a welcome cocktail for guests 21+. It's the first time the event has returned since 2018.

parkioInsight.text: Paradise Nights is an intimate event with capped attendance. If you're planning a visit to Orlando this summer and want a break from traditional theme parks, booking early tends to secure better date options before popular weekends fill up.
